# Backlog sweep plan — 2026-04-16T04:46:24Z

     (open work as of updated_at: 2026-04-16T06:00:00Z). 18 initiatives covering 48
     backlog rows. Sort: P3-correctness → P3-UX → P4-correctness → P4-misc → heroic-last.
     Within band, multiplier desc, then implementation cost asc. -->

## Project context recap

- **Priorities, strict order:** correctness → performance → SRP.
- **No backward compatibility requirements.** Breaking changes acceptable.
- **Bootstrap caveat.** This plan targets the Roslyn MCP server itself. Execution
  sessions use `Edit` + `Bash: dotnet build` + `compile_check`, NOT
  `*_apply`/`*_preview` self-application. See `self-edit-bootstrap-mode-mcp-development`
  in backlog and the qualitative session notes.
- **Companion files:** `state.json` (operational status) in this directory;
  execute with `ai_docs/prompts/backlog-sweep-execute.md`.

## Coverage summary

| Status | Count | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Active initiatives** | 18 | Closes 48 backlog rows (1 P2 · 11 P3 · 36 P4) |
| **Deferred — investigation** | 1 | `concurrent-mcp-instances-no-tools` (P3) — needs root-cause work; may not be single-PR feasible |
| **Deferred — dep-blocked** | 3 | Per Step 1 prompt rule: rows whose `deps` reference unfinished work |
| **Deferred — capability gaps** | ~16 | New tools / analyzer enhancements; not correctness-blocking — next sweep |
| **Deferred — observation/environmental** | ~10 | dr-9-* rows tagged as observations or environment notes, not server bugs |

See `## Deferred items` at the bottom for the full list with reasons.

## Initiative 1 — `formatter-format-async-wiring`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Initiative id** | `formatter-format-async-wiring` |
| **Status** | `pending` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `change-signature-preview-brace-concat-on-add-op` (P3), `dr-9-2-format-bug-001-cross-project-interface-extractio` (P4), `dr-9-3-format-bug-002-destroys-source-formatting` (P4), `dr-9-4-format-bug-003-produces-inline-itemgroup-xml` (P4), `dr-9-5-strips-the-blank-line-between-namespace-and-clas` (P4), `dr-9-6-emits-continuation-on-a-new-line-instead-of-inli` (P4), `dr-9-7-only-partially-normalizes-whitespace` (P4), `dr-9-7-produces-output-that-violates-project-formatting` (P4), `dr-9-9-format-bug-004-produces-malformed-body-closing-b` (P4), `dr-9-10-format-bug-005-renders-declaration-without-spac` (P4), `dr-9-11-format-bug-006-duplicates-leading-trivia-into-b` (P4) |
| **Diagnosis** | The `SyntaxFormatter` helper at `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Helpers/SyntaxFormatter.cs:20-54` was shipped in v1.18.2 (per CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` line 26) as **infrastructure with zero call sites**. Every preview-emitting service that uses `SyntaxFactory.*` to build new nodes still serializes via `.NormalizeWhitespace()` + `.ToFullString()` instead of routing through `Formatter.FormatAsync`. Verified at `ChangeSignatureService.PreviewAddParameterAsync` (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/ChangeSignatureService.cs:75-105`): `SyntaxFactory.Parameter(...).WithType(SyntaxFactory.ParseTypeName(request.ParameterType + " "))` is called with no formatter pass — root cause for FORMAT-BUG-005 (no-spaces declarations) and the brace-concat issue. The same anti-pattern is present across the seven services the row family enumerates. Bonus dead-code finding at `ChangeSignatureService.cs:75`: `var paramText = ...` is computed but never read (real `dead-local-variable-detector` example). |
| **Approach** | Wire `SyntaxFormatter.FormatDocumentAsync` / `ReplaceAndFormatAsync` into each preview path that synthesizes nodes: (1) `ChangeSignatureService` add/remove/rename ops, (2) `InterfaceExtractionService` cross-project + same-project, (3) `ExtractMethodService`, (4) `TypeExtractionService`, (5) `ClassSplitOrchestrator`, (6) `PackageMigrationOrchestrator` (XML side handled separately — see notes), (7) `ExtractAndWireOrchestrator` dependency-inversion path. **Pattern per service:** after building the new `SyntaxNode`, call `SyntaxFormatter.ReplaceAndFormatAsync(solution, documentId, newRoot, ct)` and use the returned `Solution`. Delete dead local `paramText` at ChangeSignatureService.cs:75. Remove the now-redundant `.NormalizeWhitespace()` calls. Note: `migrate_package_preview` produces csproj XML edits — that's handled by Item 6 (extract_type) family or unchanged here; only the C# emit paths need formatter. |
| **Scope** | ~7 services modified, ~12-15 call-site rewrites, no new files. Existing helper at `Helpers/SyntaxFormatter.cs` already has the API. |
| **Risks** | (a) Formatter may reorder usings or affect other parts of the document — `Formatter.FormatAsync` is conservative on existing code (re-parseable as no-op) but verify on each service's regression test. (b) Extract-method's existing `extract-method-preview-fabricates-this-parameter` fix (closed in v1.18.2 per CHANGELOG line 17) sits on the same code path — confirm formatter wiring doesn't regress it. (c) Cross-project interface extraction has its own `dr-9-1-emits-interface-file-without-required-directive` fix (closed v1.18.2) that depends on a semantic walker — formatter runs after, no interaction. |
| **Validation** | One regression test per closed row (10 new tests). Each test must: invoke the `_preview` tool against a fixture that triggered the format bug, assert the unified diff contains correctly-spaced output (no `publicinterfaceI`, no `signature{`, no missing space between params). Build: `dotnet build RoslynMcp.slnx -c Release -p:TreatWarningsAsErrors=true`. Full: `./eng/verify-release.ps1 -Configuration Release`. |
| **Performance review** | N/A — correctness fix, no hot-path changes. (`Formatter.FormatAsync` is per-document; previews already operate per-document.) |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Fixed` |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **Synthesized C# previews now route through `Formatter.FormatAsync` (`change-signature-preview-brace-concat-on-add-op`, `dr-9-2-format-bug-001-…`, `dr-9-3-format-bug-002-…`, `dr-9-4-format-bug-003-…`, `dr-9-5-strips-the-blank-line-…`, `dr-9-6-emits-continuation-…`, `dr-9-7-only-partially-normalizes-whitespace`, `dr-9-7-produces-output-that-violates-…`, `dr-9-9-format-bug-004-…`, `dr-9-10-format-bug-005-…`, `dr-9-11-format-bug-006-…`).** The `SyntaxFormatter` helper shipped as infrastructure in v1.18.2 had zero call sites; preview services still serialized synthesized nodes via `.NormalizeWhitespace()` + `.ToFullString()`, producing artefacts like signature-on-same-line-as-brace, missing parameter spaces, mid-base-list line breaks, and duplicated leading trivia. Wired `SyntaxFormatter.ReplaceAndFormatAsync` into `ChangeSignatureService` (add/remove/rename), `InterfaceExtractionService` (same-project + cross-project), `ExtractMethodService`, `TypeExtractionService`, `ClassSplitOrchestrator`, `ExtractAndWireOrchestrator`. Removed dead local `paramText` at `ChangeSignatureService.cs:75`. 10 regression tests covering each FORMAT-BUG-* repro. |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 11 rows. Update related: none (cross-cutting observation #1 from the v1.18.2 top-10 plan is now fully addressed). |

## Initiative 2 — `output-size-summary-mode-family`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Initiative id** | `output-size-summary-mode-family` |
| **Status** | `pending` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `find-references-preview-text-inflates-response` (P3), `symbol-impact-sweep-output-size-blowup` (P3), `get-nuget-dependencies-no-summary-mode` (P3), `get-syntax-tree-max-output-chars-incomplete-cap` (P3), `validate-workspace-output-cap-summary-mode` (P3), `dr-9-5-bug-pagination-001-has-no-pagination-filter-igno` (P4 — `test_reference_map`), `dr-9-11-payload-exceeds-mcp-tool-result-cap` (P4 — `roslyn://server/catalog`) |
| **Diagnosis** | `RefactoringService.PreviewRenameAsync` shipped a `summary=true` mode in v1.18.2 (verified at `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/RefactoringService.cs:50,101,143,156`) that drops per-file unified-diff text in favor of one-line markers — closes payload blowup on the rename path. The same pattern has NOT been applied to: `ReferenceService.FindReferencesAsync` (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/ReferenceService.cs:21-39` — `ReferenceLocationMaterializer.MaterializeDtosAsync` always produces full LineContext), `ImpactSweepService.SweepAsync` (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/ImpactSweepService.cs:34-60` — composite of refs + diagnostics + mapper callsites, no summary param), `NuGetDependencyService.GetNuGetDependenciesAsync` (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/NuGetDependencyService.cs:63-140` — emits full per-project package list), `SyntaxService.GetSyntaxTreeAsync` (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/SyntaxService.cs:17-25,35` — `maxOutputChars` is leaf-text-only, structural JSON dominates), `WorkspaceValidationService.ValidateAsync` (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/WorkspaceValidationService.cs:43-122` — emits full diagnostic detail), `TestReferenceMapService` (no pagination at all per dr-9-5), and the `roslyn://server/catalog` resource handler. |
| **Approach** | Pattern per tool: add `bool summary = false` parameter to the service interface and tool layer. When `summary=true`, return counts + filePath/line lists + per-file/per-package summary markers; drop preview/lineContext/per-diagnostic-message text. (a) `IReferenceService.FindReferencesAsync` + `ReferenceService` — pass summary through `MaterializeDtosAsync`. (b) `IImpactSweepService.SweepAsync` + `ImpactSweepService` — propagate to references and persistence-layer findings. (c) `INuGetDependencyService.GetNuGetDependenciesAsync` — return per-package counts + project references only. (d) `ISyntaxService.GetSyntaxTreeAsync` — enforce hard `maxTotalBytes` cap that aborts serialization with `truncated=true`; add `maxNodes` param. (e) `IWorkspaceValidationService.ValidateAsync` — return `{overallStatus, counts, perProjectSummary[]}` only. (f) `TestReferenceMapService` — add `offset`/`limit` pagination + honor `projectName` filter. (g) `roslyn://server/catalog` resource — paginate prompt/tool list. Tool-schema updates for each. |
| **Scope** | 6 services + 1 resource modified, 7 tool-schema updates, 0 new files. `ReferenceLocationMaterializer` may need a summary-aware variant. |
| **Risks** | (a) Existing callers expect rich responses — but per project-policy "no backward compatibility required," default `summary=false` preserves current shape. (b) Stress-test Recommendation #1 (per backlog row text) wanted these consolidated — this initiative IS that consolidation. (c) `validate_workspace summary=true` interacts with the test-discovery stage — keep `discoveredTests`/`testRunResult` arrays present but trim per-test detail. |
| **Validation** | One regression test per closed row (7 new tests): each test invokes the tool against a high-fan-out fixture (Jellyfin-class symbol, 40-project solution, 7000-line file), asserts response size with `summary=true` is <10KB and assert that `summary=false` still works (default behavior preserved). Stress-test scaffolding already exists at `tests/RoslynMcp.IntegrationTests/...`. |
| **Performance review** | **Hot path — measure.** Baseline (from `ai_docs/audit-reports/20260415T193603Z_jellyfin_stress-test.md`): `find_references` 154 KB on 233-ref symbol; `symbol_impact_sweep` 886 KB on 1452-ref `BaseItem`; `get_nuget_dependencies` 102 KB on 40-project solution; `get_syntax_tree` 229 KB on 7889-line file with `maxOutputChars=20000`; `validate_workspace` 135 KB on 40-project solution. Expected post-fix with `summary=true`: ≤10 KB for each. Cite via response-size assertion in regression tests. |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Added` |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **`summary=true` mode added to 6 high-fan-out tools + pagination on `test_reference_map` (`find-references-preview-text-inflates-response`, `symbol-impact-sweep-output-size-blowup`, `get-nuget-dependencies-no-summary-mode`, `get-syntax-tree-max-output-chars-incomplete-cap`, `validate-workspace-output-cap-summary-mode`, `dr-9-5-bug-pagination-001-…`, `dr-9-11-payload-exceeds-mcp-tool-result-cap`).** Each tool produced 100KB+ payloads on representative real workloads (154KB / 886KB / 102KB / 229KB / 135KB on Jellyfin) and silently exceeded the MCP token cap. Following the v1.18.2 `rename_preview summary=true` pattern, added optional `summary: bool = false` to: `find_references`, `symbol_impact_sweep`, `get_nuget_dependencies`, `validate_workspace`. `get_syntax_tree` gains a hard `maxTotalBytes` cap + `maxNodes` parameter (the previous `maxOutputChars` documented as leaf-text-only is preserved). `test_reference_map` gains `offset`/`limit` pagination and now honors `projectName`. `roslyn://server/catalog` resource paginated. With `summary=true`, response payloads drop to ≤10 KB on the same workloads. |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 7 rows. Update related: stress-test Recommendation #1 in `ai_docs/audit-reports/20260415T193603Z_jellyfin_stress-test.md` is fully delivered — note this in the PR description. |

## Initiative 3 — `workspace-readiness-contract`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Initiative id** | `workspace-readiness-contract` |
| **Status** | `pending` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `dr-9-7-reports-during-refactoring-transitions-but-the-w` (P3), `severity-flag-soft-inconsistency-does-not-block-work` (P4), `workspace-load-isready-misreports-unresolved-analyzers` (P4), `dr-9-9-response-claims` (P4) |
| **Diagnosis** | `WorkspaceStatusSummaryDto.From` at `src/RoslynMcp.Core/Models/WorkspaceStatusSummaryDto.cs:52` computes `isReady = status.IsLoaded && !status.IsStale && errors == 0`. Two failure modes: (a) `WORKSPACE_UNRESOLVED_ANALYZER` is classified as a **warning**, not an error — so `isReady=true` even when 40 analyzer-load failures mean every analyzer-driven tool will silently miss findings (Jellyfin §1 evidence). (b) The transient-stale window during `*_apply` → `workspace_reload` flips `IsStale=true` briefly — `isReady` reads `false` while tools still operate (NetworkDocumentation §9.7 evidence). The `dr-9-9-response-claims` row reports `workspace_close` returning `staleAction: "auto-reloaded"` even though no reload occurred — a separate logic bug in the close path. |
| **Approach** | (1) Add `analyzersReady: bool` field to `WorkspaceStatusSummaryDto`. Compute by counting workspace diagnostics with id `WORKSPACE_UNRESOLVED_ANALYZER`. Demote `isReady` to `analyzersReady && IsLoaded && !IsStale && errors == 0` so the bit means "everything will work". (2) Add `restoreHint` payload distinguishing "transient stale during refactor (retry in Nms)" from "broken-and-tools-fail" — emit different hints based on whether `_changeTracker` shows an in-flight apply. (3) `WorkspaceClose` path: only emit `staleAction: "auto-reloaded"` when an actual reload ran. Search for the close-path emission and gate on a `WasReloaded` boolean. (4) Update `workspace_status` / `workspace_list` / `workspace_load` tool docs to describe the new contract. |
| **Scope** | `WorkspaceStatusSummaryDto.cs` + `WorkspaceManager.cs` (close path) + `WorkspaceTools.cs` (response shape) + 1-2 supporting helpers. ~5-8 files. |
| **Risks** | (a) `analyzersReady` is a new field; agents currently parsing `isReady` get a tighter signal — that's the goal but flag it in CHANGELOG breaking-changes section. (b) Demoting `isReady` based on analyzer warnings may cause tools to refuse work that previously "succeeded silently" — this is the correct behavior. (c) Verify the close-path `staleAction` claim against `WorkspaceManager.CloseAsync` — may be threading through CompositePreviewStore or change-tracker. |
| **Validation** | 4 regression tests: (1) load workspace with intentional unresolved analyzer reference; assert `isReady=false`, `analyzersReady=false`, restoreHint mentions analyzer build. (2) Mid-apply stale state assertion via test-only injection; assert restoreHint says "retry transient stale". (3) `workspace_close` without reload assertion; verify `staleAction` field absent or null. (4) Backwards-compat: existing healthy workspace returns `isReady=true && analyzersReady=true`. |
| **Performance review** | N/A — correctness fix, no hot-path changes. |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Changed — BREAKING` |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **`isReady` contract tightened; new `analyzersReady` field; `workspace_close` no longer falsely reports auto-reload (`dr-9-7-reports-during-refactoring-transitions-but-the-w`, `severity-flag-soft-inconsistency-does-not-block-work`, `workspace-load-isready-misreports-unresolved-analyzers`, `dr-9-9-response-claims`).** `WorkspaceStatusSummaryDto.From` (`src/RoslynMcp.Core/Models/WorkspaceStatusSummaryDto.cs:52`) computed `isReady` only from `errors == 0`, which let `WORKSPACE_UNRESOLVED_ANALYZER` warnings pass while every analyzer-driven tool would silently miss findings (Jellyfin 2026-04-16 reproduction: `isReady=true` with 40 analyzer warnings). Added `analyzersReady: bool` (false when any `WORKSPACE_UNRESOLVED_ANALYZER` is present); `isReady` now requires both. Transient-stale during refactor flips `restoreHint` to a "retry in Nms" hint instead of leaving `isReady=false` indistinguishable from broken state. `workspace_close` no longer emits `staleAction: "auto-reloaded"` when no reload occurred (NetworkDocumentation §9.7 + SampleSolution §9.8). |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 4 rows. |

## Initiative 4 — `extract-type-apply-correctness`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Initiative id** | `extract-type-apply-correctness` |
| **Status** | `pending` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `dr-9-1-does-not-update-external-consumer-call-sites` (P3), `dr-9-3-preserves-when-new-type-does-not-inherit-the-bas` (P4) |
| **Diagnosis** | `TypeExtractionService` apply path (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/TypeExtractionService.cs`) has two correctness gaps: (a) When extracted members are public, external consumers (e.g., `SampleApp/Program.cs` referencing the original type) are not rewritten to point at the new type — preview shows clean diff, apply produces broken consumer code (SampleSolution §9.1 evidence). (b) When the extracted type does NOT inherit from the source's base, the `override` keyword is preserved on extracted methods producing `CS0115: no suitable method to override` (IT-Chat-Bot §9.3 evidence). |
| **Approach** | (a) In `TypeExtractionService.ExtractTypeApplyAsync`, after the new type is created, run a project-wide reference scan for the extracted symbols (use `SymbolFinder.FindReferencesAsync`). For consumers outside the source file, rewrite identifiers to qualify with the new type (or add `using` if needed). Match the existing pattern in `bulk_replace_type` for the rewrite primitive. (b) When emitting the new type, walk extracted method declarations and strip the `override` modifier IF the new type's `BaseType` differs from the source's. Use `Modifier.Override` / `SyntaxFactory.TokenList(...)` to filter. |
| **Scope** | `TypeExtractionService.cs` (apply path) + 1-2 helper additions. ~3 files modified. |
| **Risks** | (a) Aggressive consumer rewrite could break consumers that explicitly accessed the source type's other (non-extracted) members — only rewrite identifier references that resolve to extracted members. (b) `override` stripping must check `IsOverride` AND base-type compatibility, not just blindly strip. (c) Verify interaction with already-shipped `dr-9-2-leaves-duplicate-type-in-source-file-and-creates` fix (closed v1.18.2) — that fix moved member declarations cleanly, this one extends to consumer call sites. |
| **Validation** | 2 regression tests: (1) Extract public method from `Type1` → `Type2` with external consumer referencing `Type1.Method()`; assert apply rewrites consumer to `Type2.Method()`. (2) Extract method that overrides base from a type whose extracted form does NOT inherit; assert `override` keyword stripped, no CS0115. |
| **Performance review** | N/A — correctness fix. (Reference scan adds one project-wide query but only runs on apply, not preview.) |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Fixed` |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **`extract_type_apply` rewrites external consumers and prunes invalid `override` modifiers (`dr-9-1-does-not-update-external-consumer-call-sites`, `dr-9-3-preserves-when-new-type-does-not-inherit-the-bas`).** Two correctness gaps in `TypeExtractionService` apply path: (a) Consumers outside the source file kept calling the original type's now-removed members (SampleSolution §9.1 evidence; preview was clean, apply broke `SampleApp/Program.cs`). Apply now runs a project-wide `SymbolFinder.FindReferencesAsync` scan for extracted symbols and rewrites consumer identifiers to qualify against the new type. (b) Extracted methods retained `override` even when the new type didn't inherit from the same base, producing `CS0115` (IT-Chat-Bot §9.3 evidence). Apply now walks extracted method declarations, compares `BaseType` between source and target, and strips `override` when incompatible. |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 2 rows. |

## Initiative 5 — `format-range-preview-empty-diff`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Initiative id** | `format-range-preview-empty-diff` |
| **Status** | `pending` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `format-range-preview-empty-diff-compile-check-filter-false-clean` (P3 — Defect A only; Defect B was closed in v1.18.2 by `compile-check-zero-projects-claimed-success-after-reload`), `dr-9-12-flag-format-range-empty-returns-empty-diff-on-d` (P4) |
| **Diagnosis** | `FormatVerifyService` / `format_range_preview` returns a `unifiedDiff` with header but no `@@` hunks even when the apply path will mutate the file (NetworkDocumentation §9.5 Defect A evidence). Verified the v1.18.2 `compile-check-zero-projects-claimed-success-after-reload` fix already addressed Defect B (compile_check filter false-clean) — that's why this initiative trims to Defect A only. **Verify on execution** that `format_range_preview` still produces empty diffs on dirty input by re-running the NetworkDocumentation repro. |
| **Approach** | Investigate `FormatService.PreviewRangeAsync` (or `FormatVerifyService` — exact location TBD by execution session). Likely cause: range bounds passed to `Formatter.FormatAsync(document, range)` don't include trailing trivia where the formatter's edits land — diff calculation runs on the wrong range. Fix: extend the range to the next significant token boundary, OR run the formatter on the whole document and intersect-diff against the requested range. Validate the diff against actual apply-path output. |
| **Scope** | `FormatVerifyService.cs` + tests. ~2-3 files. |
| **Risks** | If the row is no longer reproducible (Defect A may have been incidentally fixed by the v1.18.2 compile_check work), drop and mark obsolete per executor Step 4. |
| **Validation** | 1 regression test: dirty fixture with range that requires inter-line whitespace fix; assert preview's `unifiedDiff` matches the apply mutation. Per Step 4 of executor, re-verify reproducibility before implementing. |
| **Performance review** | N/A — correctness fix. |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Fixed` |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **`format_range_preview` no longer returns empty diff for dirty input (`format-range-preview-empty-diff-compile-check-filter-false-clean`, `dr-9-12-flag-format-range-empty-returns-empty-diff-on-d`).** When the requested range did not include trailing trivia where the formatter's edits landed, the preview diff was empty while apply still mutated the file (NetworkDocumentation §9.5). Fixed by extending range bounds to the next significant token boundary before computing the diff so preview and apply produce identical output. (Note: the row's Defect B — `compile_check` filter false-clean — was previously closed in v1.18.2 by `compile-check-zero-projects-claimed-success-after-reload`.) |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 2 rows. Note Defect B already closed in v1.18.2. |

## Initiative 6 — `change-signature-preview-ergonomics`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Initiative id** | `change-signature-preview-ergonomics` |
| **Status** | `pending` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `change-signature-preview-callsite-summary` (P3), `change-signature-parameter-span-hint-for-remove` (P4) |
| **Diagnosis** | (a) `ChangeSignatureService.PreviewRemoveParameterAsync` (verified at `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/ChangeSignatureService.cs:108-120`) issues a `previewToken` whose `unifiedDiff` contains only the interface-owner file. The 4 callsite files that the apply path actually rewrites (per firewall-analyzer 2026-04-15 evidence) are invisible in the preview — agents can't verify the tool will do the right thing. Compare `RenameService.PreviewAsync` which already enumerates all affected files. (b) `op=remove` requires `name` or `position` to identify the parameter — there's no path from "caret on parameter X" → the right combo. |
| **Approach** | (a) `ChangeSignatureService.BuildPreviewDto` enumerates `accumulator` for declaration changes only — extend to also include callsite files. Surface as `callsiteUpdates: {filePath, callsiteCount}[]` minimum (full diffs preferred but optional for payload size). Match the response shape of `RenameService.PreviewAsync`. (b) Add `parameterLine`/`parameterColumn` to `SymbolLocator` or as alternative locator — when caret is on a parameter token, dispatch to remove handler with auto-detected `name`/`position`. Extend `SymbolLocatorFactory.Create` to accept the parameter-position locator. |
| **Scope** | `ChangeSignatureService.cs` + `SymbolLocatorFactory.cs` + tool schema + 1-2 helpers. ~3-4 files. |
| **Risks** | (a) Including all callsite diffs may inflate response size — apply the v1.18.2 `summary=true` pattern from RefactoringService to suppress per-file diffs when fan-out is high. Defer to Initiative 2 if scope becomes large. (b) Parameter-position locator could collide with method-name locator for inline lambdas — disambiguate by checking if the resolved symbol is `IParameterSymbol`. |
| **Validation** | 2 regression tests: (1) `op=remove` on cross-file interface method with 4 implementing classes → preview enumerates all 5 files (interface + 4 impls). (2) Caret on parameter name → resolves to remove with correct `position`. |
| **Performance review** | N/A — correctness fix. |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Fixed` (callsite enumeration) + `Added` (parameter locator) |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **`change_signature_preview` enumerates all affected callsite files; parameter-position locator added (`change-signature-preview-callsite-summary`, `change-signature-parameter-span-hint-for-remove`).** `op=remove` previews exposed only the interface-owner file's diff while apply rewrote 4 callsite files invisibly (firewall-analyzer 2026-04-15 evidence — agents abandoned the preview and used `Edit` instead). `BuildPreviewDto` now includes per-callsite-file entries matching `RenameService.PreviewAsync` response shape. Added `parameterLine`/`parameterColumn` locator option so a caret on a parameter token dispatches to remove with auto-detected `name`/`position` — eliminates the "guess 0-based position" papercut. |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 2 rows. |

## Initiative 7 — `error-clarity-tool-and-resource-paths`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Initiative id** | `error-clarity-tool-and-resource-paths` |
| **Status** | `pending` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `mcp-parameter-validation-error-messages` (P3), `dr-9-13-flag-resource-invalid-range-resource-returns-ge` (P4), `dr-9-7-bug-json-parse-surfaces-stack-trace` (P4) |
| **Diagnosis** | `ToolErrorHandler` (`src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Tools/ToolErrorHandler.cs`) HAS handlers for `InnerException is JsonException` (line 218-223) and `ArgumentException with "Invocation"` (line 224-228) — but the path may not be triggering for SDK invocation envelope. **Verify on execution:** trace the exact exception chain produced when (a) a tool is called with a missing required parameter, (b) a resource is called with an invalid range, (c) `get_prompt_text` is called with malformed JSON parameters. Adjust the `ClassifyError` walker to catch the actual exception types the SDK produces. The MCP SDK invocation wrapper at `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Program.cs` (or equivalent) is the boundary — may need to wrap tool registrations to route through `ToolErrorHandler` consistently. |
| **Approach** | (1) Run a repro: invoke a tool with missing `workspaceId`, capture the exact exception type/chain. (2) If the type is `McpException` or similar, add a handler in `ToolErrorHandler.ErrorHandlers` dictionary. (3) For resources: ensure all resource handlers route through `ToolErrorHandler.ExecuteResource` (verify all handlers in `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Resources/`). (4) `get_prompt_text` JSON parse surfaces stack trace — wrap the prompt-rendering path in try/catch that emits structured `InvalidArgument` instead of bubbling `JsonReaderException`. |
| **Scope** | `ToolErrorHandler.cs` (extend handlers) + `ServerResources.cs` / `WorkspaceResources.cs` (route through `ExecuteResource`) + `PromptShimTools.cs` (wrap parse) + 3-5 tool-registration sites if SDK boundary needs wrapping. ~5-8 files. |
| **Risks** | (a) Existing `-32603` errors that DO classify correctly (FileNotFoundException, etc.) must keep their structured shape — don't over-broaden. (b) Some MCP SDK invocation paths may bypass our wrappers entirely — surface those as a separate dr-9-* style row if found. |
| **Validation** | 3 regression tests: (1) Call tool with missing required parameter; assert response is structured JSON with `category: "InvalidArgument"`, `message` names the missing parameter. (2) Call `roslyn://workspace/{id}/file/{path}` resource with invalid range; assert structured error, not `-32603`. (3) Call `get_prompt_text` with malformed `parametersJson`; assert structured `InvalidArgument`, not `JsonReaderException` stack. |
| **Performance review** | N/A — error-path fix only. |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Fixed` |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **Tool and resource invocation errors return structured payloads instead of generic `-32603` (`mcp-parameter-validation-error-messages`, `dr-9-13-flag-resource-invalid-range-…`, `dr-9-7-bug-json-parse-surfaces-stack-trace`).** Missing/invalid parameters surfaced as `An error occurred invoking '<tool>'` across 13 tools + 8 prompts (NetworkDocumentation + IT-Chat-Bot 2026-04-13 evidence — biggest debug-friction single-fix). Extended `ToolErrorHandler.ClassifyError` to catch the SDK's actual invocation-envelope exception types and unwrap to `InvalidArgument` with parameter name. `roslyn://workspace/{id}/file/{path}` resource handler now routes through `ToolErrorHandler.ExecuteResource` so invalid-range errors return structured JSON. `get_prompt_text` wraps `JsonReaderException` from malformed `parametersJson` and emits structured `InvalidArgument` instead of bubbling the stack trace. |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 3 rows. |

---

## Initiative 8 — `code-fix-provider-loading`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Initiative id** | `code-fix-provider-loading` |
| **Status** | `pending` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `dr-9-2-no-code-fix-providers-loaded-for-any-exercised-d` (P4), `dr-9-4-returns-for-some-ide-series-diagnostic-ids` (P4), `dr-9-5-returns-silent-empty-result-for-info-severity-di` (P4), `severity-flag-observable-tool-behaviour-is-inconsist` (P4) |
| **Diagnosis** | `CodeFixProviderRegistry` (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/CodeFixProviderRegistry.cs`) loads code-fix providers but coverage gaps: no providers for several IDE-series + CA-series diagnostic IDs, and `fix_all_preview` returns silent empty result when no provider exists. Symptom: caller cannot distinguish "no occurrences in scope" from "no provider loaded for this id" (severity-flag observation). |
| **Approach** | (1) Audit which assemblies provide IDE/CA code-fix providers — `Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Features` for IDE*, `Microsoft.CodeQuality.Analyzers` for CA* — confirm package references in `RoslynMcp.Roslyn.csproj`. (2) `FixAllService.PreviewAsync` (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/FixAllService.cs`): when no provider matches the diagnosticId, return a `guidanceMessage` field describing the absence. Don't conflate it with "matched 0 occurrences." (3) For `Info`-severity diagnostics that have a provider but no actionable fix, return `guidanceMessage` explaining the diagnostic is informational. |
| **Scope** | `CodeFixProviderRegistry.cs` + `FixAllService.cs` + project file (package adds if needed) + tool schema. ~3-5 files. |
| **Risks** | (a) Adding analyzer packages may inflate startup time — measure first; consider lazy loading. (b) IDE0005 already has special handling per the existing `fix_all_preview` `guidanceMessage` field — extend the pattern to other ids without breaking the IDE0005 path. |
| **Validation** | 4 regression tests: (1) `fix_all_preview` for diagnostic with no provider → response includes `guidanceMessage: "no code fix provider loaded for {id}"`. (2) `fix_all_preview` for `Info`-severity diagnostic without actionable fix → distinct `guidanceMessage`. (3) Exercise CA1000 and IDE0044 to confirm new providers loaded. (4) Existing IDE0005 path unchanged. |
| **Performance review** | **Touched startup path — measure if package added.** Baseline: `workspace_load` cold start ~10s on Jellyfin. If a new analyzer package is added, re-measure and ensure no >20% regression; if so, lazy-load. |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Fixed` + possibly `Added` (if packages) |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **`fix_all_preview` distinguishes "no provider loaded" from "no occurrences" + IDE/CA provider coverage extended (`dr-9-2-no-code-fix-providers-loaded-…`, `dr-9-4-returns-for-some-ide-series-diagnostic-ids`, `dr-9-5-returns-silent-empty-result-for-info-severity-di`, `severity-flag-observable-tool-behaviour-is-inconsist`).** Missing code-fix providers for several IDE/CA diagnostic IDs caused `fix_all_preview` to return empty results indistinguishable from "no occurrences" — observable behaviour was inconsistent per the audit severity matrix. `CodeFixProviderRegistry` now loads `Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Features` + `Microsoft.CodeQuality.Analyzers` providers; `FixAllService` returns a `guidanceMessage` when no provider matches the diagnostic id, separately when an `Info`-severity diagnostic has no actionable fix. |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 4 rows. |

---

## Initiative 9 — `symbol-resolver-hardening`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Initiative id** | `symbol-resolver-hardening` |
| **Status** | `pending` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `symbol-impact-sweep-race-with-auto-reload` (P4), `symbol-info-lenient-whitespace-resolution` (P4), `symbol-search-partial-match-gap` (P4) |
| **Diagnosis** | Three independent but related symbol-resolution defects: (a) `SymbolImpactSweepService` errors with "No symbol could be resolved" during workspace auto-reload — misleading because the symbol exists, the workspace is just transiently in flux. (b) `SymbolInfoService` lenient position resolution: `(line 31, col 1)` on a blank line silently returns the adjacent declaration instead of "no symbol" — masks caller bugs. (c) `SymbolSearchService.SearchAsync` returns empty for valid partial names (e.g., "ConversationManager" → empty when `ITChatBot.Conversation.ConversationManager` exists). |
| **Approach** | (a) `SymbolImpactSweepService.SweepAsync` — wrap `_workspace.GetCurrentSolution(workspaceId)` in a try-catch that detects the auto-reload-in-progress state (use `WorkspaceSession.IsReloading` flag added if not present) and returns `"workspace is reloading, retry in Nms"` envelope. (b) `SymbolInfoService` — add `allowAdjacent: bool = false` parameter; when false, return null with "no symbol at (L,C); nearest is X at (L',C')" hint. Default opt-in behavior preserves "lenient" current behavior unless callers opt out — but for new sweep-style usage this is too lax. **Recommend default flip to `allowAdjacent=false` (BREAKING — gates lenient mode behind opt-in).** (c) `SymbolSearchService.SearchAsync` — investigate the substring-match scope: probably narrow to declared-name-substring only, missing namespace-qualified prefixes. Fix to also match against `SymbolDisplayFormat.QualifiedNameOnlyFormat`. |
| **Scope** | `ImpactSweepService.cs` + `SymbolNavigationService.cs` (which contains SymbolInfo path) + `SymbolSearchService.cs` + `WorkspaceSession` reload-flag if not present. ~4-5 files. |
| **Risks** | (a) `allowAdjacent` default flip is breaking — call out in CHANGELOG. (b) Race-condition retry envelope must include actual elapsed-since-reload-start to set "Nms" hint accurately. (c) `SymbolSearch` partial match may now return more results — verify `limit` param caps correctly. |
| **Validation** | 3 regression tests: (1) Trigger workspace_reload mid-flight; concurrently invoke `symbol_impact_sweep`; assert envelope says "reloading, retry". (2) Call `symbol_info` at blank line with `allowAdjacent=false`; assert null + nearest-symbol hint. (3) Call `symbol_search` for fragment matching a fully-qualified name; assert match returned. |
| **Performance review** | N/A — correctness fix. |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Fixed` + `Changed — BREAKING` (allowAdjacent default flip) |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **Symbol resolver hardened: race-aware errors, lenient mode opt-in, partial-name match (`symbol-impact-sweep-race-with-auto-reload`, `symbol-info-lenient-whitespace-resolution`, `symbol-search-partial-match-gap`).** Three resolver defects masking caller bugs: `symbol_impact_sweep` reported "no symbol resolved" during workspace auto-reload (Jellyfin §9), `symbol_info` at a blank line silently returned the nearest adjacent declaration (Jellyfin stress test §7), and `symbol_search` returned empty for valid partial names that included a namespace prefix (IT-Chat-Bot §9.5). Race state now returns `"workspace is reloading, retry in Nms"` envelope. `symbol_info` adds `allowAdjacent: bool` (default `false` — BREAKING; previous lenient behavior is now opt-in) and emits `"no symbol at (L,C); nearest is X at (L',C')"` when not adjacent. `symbol_search` substring matcher now matches against fully-qualified names. |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 3 rows. |

---

## Initiative 10 — `preview-token-cross-coupling`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Initiative id** | `preview-token-cross-coupling` |
| **Status** | `pending` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `dr-9-6-preview-token-issued-in-turn-t-invalidated-by-a` (P4), `severity-flag-unexpected-coupling-of-preview-tokens` (P4) |
| **Diagnosis** | `PreviewStore` (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/PreviewStore.cs`) shares a single solution snapshot across all stored previews — a sibling `*_apply` in the same turn invalidates other tokens silently. NetworkDocumentation §9.6 evidence: undocumented cross-token coupling. |
| **Approach** | Per-token snapshot isolation: `PreviewStore.Store` captures the workspace version + solution snapshot at the time of preview. `PreviewStore.Retrieve` validates the stored token's snapshot version against the current workspace version. If a sibling apply has bumped the version, the retrieved token includes a `staleness: { reason: "sibling-apply-invalidated", siblingPrUrl: ..., reapplyToken: ... }` field. Callers can either re-preview or force apply with `force: true` (already exists per the `DiffTruncated` infrastructure shipped in v1.18.2). |
| **Scope** | `PreviewStore.cs` + `IPreviewStore` interface + `RefactoringService.ApplyRefactoringAsync` (consume staleness signal) + tool error path (surface staleness). ~4-5 files. |
| **Risks** | (a) Per-token snapshot doubles `PreviewStore` memory — bound by existing eviction (verify with stress test). (b) The "force apply against stale snapshot" path may now produce silently-wrong edits — gate behind explicit `force: true` AND require `acknowledged: "sibling-apply-invalidated"` in the apply payload. |
| **Validation** | 2 regression tests: (1) Issue two previews in turn T; apply one; retrieve the other → response includes `staleness` info. (2) Apply with `force: true` after staleness signal → success but logs warning. |
| **Performance review** | N/A — correctness fix. PreviewStore memory bounded. |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Fixed` + `Changed — BREAKING` (preview retrieval contract) |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **`PreviewStore` per-token snapshot isolation; sibling-apply staleness surfaced (`dr-9-6-preview-token-issued-in-turn-t-invalidated-by-a`, `severity-flag-unexpected-coupling-of-preview-tokens`).** A single shared snapshot meant a sibling `*_apply` in the same turn silently invalidated other preview tokens (NetworkDocumentation §9.6). `PreviewStore.Store` now captures workspace version per-token; `Retrieve` returns a `staleness: { reason, siblingPrUrl, reapplyToken }` field when the snapshot is older than the current workspace version. Apply path requires explicit `force: true` AND `acknowledged: "sibling-apply-invalidated"` to apply a stale preview. |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 2 rows. |

---

## Initiative 11 — `server-info-update-latest-inverted`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Initiative id** | `server-info-update-latest-inverted` |
| **Status** | `pending` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `server-info-update-latest-inverted` (P4) |
| **Diagnosis** | `server_info` reports `latest=1.16.0` while `current=1.18.2` — `latest` field semantics are inverted (Jellyfin §1 evidence). `ServerTools.cs` likely computes `latest` from a NuGet registry lookup but doesn't take `max(current, registryLatest)`. |
| **Approach** | In `ServerTools.GetServerInfoAsync` (or the registry lookup helper), compute `latest = registryLatest > current ? registryLatest : null` (or always `max(current, registryLatest)` if you want non-null). Document field semantics in the schema. |
| **Scope** | `ServerTools.cs` + 1 helper. 2 files. |
| **Risks** | None — trivial fix. |
| **Validation** | 1 regression test: mock registry returning lower version than current; assert `latest` is null (or current). |
| **Performance review** | N/A. |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Fixed` |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **`server_info.update.latest` no longer inverts when current > registry (`server-info-update-latest-inverted`).** Field reported `latest=1.16.0` while `current=1.18.2` (Jellyfin 2026-04-16). Now computes `latest = max(current, registryLatest)` (or null when no newer version exists). Field semantics clarified in the schema. |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 1 row. |

## Initiative 12 — `mcp-stdio-console-flush-on-exit`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Initiative id** | `mcp-stdio-console-flush-on-exit` |
| **Status** | `pending` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `mcp-stdio-console-flush-on-exit` (P4) |
| **Diagnosis** | `Program.cs` stdio lifecycle exits without `Console.Out.Flush()` — clients receive 0 bytes when stdin closes (IT-Chat-Bot §9.4). |
| **Approach** | In the stdio shutdown path of `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Program.cs`, add `Console.Out.Flush()` before exit. Hook on `AppDomain.CurrentDomain.ProcessExit` and on the stdin-EOF detection branch. |
| **Scope** | `Program.cs`. 1 file. |
| **Risks** | None — additive. |
| **Validation** | 1 regression test: pipe `echo '{}' | roslynmcp` and assert stdout has content (currently empty per the audit). |
| **Performance review** | N/A. |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Fixed` |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **stdout flushed on stdin-EOF / process exit (`mcp-stdio-console-flush-on-exit`).** When stdin closed (bash-pipe integration pattern), the server exited without flushing pending stdout — clients received 0 bytes (IT-Chat-Bot 2026-04-13 §9.4). Added `Console.Out.Flush()` to `Program.cs` shutdown path on both `ProcessExit` and stdin-EOF branches. |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 1 row. |

Fields now defensively populated with synthesized fallback if Roslyn returns null. |

## Initiative 14 — `file-resource-uri-windows-path-handling`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Initiative id** | `file-resource-uri-windows-path-handling` |
| **Status** | `pending` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `file-resource-uri-windows-path-handling` (P4) |
| **Diagnosis** | `roslyn://workspace/{id}/file/{filePath}` resource URI fails on Windows because absolute-path `filePath` segment contains `:` and backslashes that conflict with URI grammar (IT-Chat-Bot §14). |
| **Approach** | In the resource URI handler (`src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Resources/WorkspaceResources.cs`), normalize the `filePath` segment via `Uri.EscapeDataString` on outbound URIs and `Uri.UnescapeDataString` on inbound, OR use base64url encoding for Windows paths. Document the convention in the resource schema. |
| **Scope** | `WorkspaceResources.cs` + helper + tests. 2-3 files. |
| **Risks** | (a) Existing clients sending unescaped paths — accept both shapes for one transition: try unescaped first, then escaped. (b) Cross-platform: ensure forward-slash paths still work on Linux/macOS. |
| **Validation** | 2 regression tests: (1) Windows path `C:\Code-Repo\foo.cs` resource lookup → resolves correctly. (2) Forward-slash path `/home/user/foo.cs` still works. |
| **Performance review** | N/A. |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Fixed` |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **`roslyn://workspace/{id}/file/{filePath}` resource URI handles Windows paths (`file-resource-uri-windows-path-handling`).** Absolute Windows paths with `:` and backslashes conflicted with URI grammar (IT-Chat-Bot 2026-04-13 §14). Resource handler now URI-encodes path segments outbound and decodes inbound; both encoded and unencoded paths accepted. |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 1 row. |

## Initiative 16 — `response-key-casing-standardization`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Initiative id** | `response-key-casing-standardization` |
| **Status** | `pending` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `response-key-casing-standardization` (P4) |
| **Diagnosis** | Tool response JSON uses PascalCase keys (`WorkspaceId`, `PreviewToken`) while input parameters use camelCase; mixed across tools (IT-Chat-Bot §18 + §9.8). `test_related` and `source_generated_documents` omit top-level `_meta`. |
| **Approach** | Standardize on camelCase for all responses. Configure `JsonSerializerOptions` at the host boundary with `PropertyNamingPolicy = JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase` (already done for `MetaSerializer.CamelCase` per `ToolErrorHandler.cs:10-14`). Audit all tool response DTO emission sites and switch to the global serializer. Ensure `test_related` and `source_generated_documents` route through `ToolErrorHandler.ExecuteAsync` (which injects `_meta`). |
| **Scope** | `JsonDefaults.cs` (or global serializer) + tool registration sites + `TestReferenceMapTools` (likely) + `SyntaxTools` (source-gen docs) + tests. ~5-8 files. |
| **Risks** | **BREAKING** — clients parsing PascalCase keys will fail. Acceptable per project policy. Call out prominently in CHANGELOG. |
| **Validation** | 2 regression tests: (1) Snapshot tests for one tool of each previously-PascalCase response — assert all keys are camelCase. (2) `test_related` and `source_generated_documents` include `_meta` block. |
| **Performance review** | N/A. |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Changed — BREAKING` |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **All tool response keys standardized to camelCase; `_meta` present on every read-tool response (`response-key-casing-standardization`).** Tool responses inconsistently used PascalCase (`WorkspaceId`, `PreviewToken`) while inputs used camelCase (IT-Chat-Bot 2026-04-13 §18 + §9.8). All response emission sites now route through the global camelCase `JsonSerializerOptions`. `test_related` and `source_generated_documents` now route through `ToolErrorHandler.ExecuteAsync` so they include the `_meta` timing block. **BREAKING** — clients parsing PascalCase keys must update. |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 1 row. |

## Initiative 17 — `validate-workspace-fabricated-paths`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Initiative id** | `validate-workspace-fabricated-paths` |
| **Status** | `pending` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `dr-9-8-bug-validate-fabricated-accepts-fabricated-silen` (P4) |
| **Diagnosis** | `WorkspaceValidationService.ValidateAsync` (verified at `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/WorkspaceValidationService.cs:43-122`) accepts `changedFilePaths` without verifying file existence — fabricated paths silently produce empty test discovery (firewall-analyzer §9.8). |
| **Approach** | Add file-existence check at `WorkspaceValidationService.ResolveChangedFiles` (line 129-140). For each path in `caller`: verify `File.Exists(path)`; if missing, log warning and surface in response as `unknownFilePaths: string[]`. Test discovery proceeds with the known subset; response includes a structured warning if the unknown set is non-empty. |
| **Scope** | `WorkspaceValidationService.cs` + DTO update + test. 3 files. |
| **Risks** | (a) Files that exist in the workspace but not on disk (e.g., source-generated) may false-positive — check both `File.Exists` AND `solution.GetDocumentIdsWithFilePath(path)`. (b) Existing callers passing non-existent paths now get warnings — acceptable, surfaces silent bugs. |
| **Validation** | 1 regression test: invoke `validate_workspace` with mix of real + fabricated paths; assert response includes `unknownFilePaths` listing the fabricated ones, real paths still discovered. |
| **Performance review** | N/A — single `File.Exists` per path is negligible. |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Fixed` |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **`validate_workspace` flags fabricated `changedFilePaths` (`dr-9-8-bug-validate-fabricated-accepts-fabricated-silen`).** Previously accepted any path silently — fabricated paths produced empty test discovery with no warning (firewall-analyzer 2026-04-15 §9.8). Response now includes `unknownFilePaths: string[]` listing paths that don't exist on disk AND aren't workspace documents (covers source-generated files). Test discovery proceeds with the known subset. |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 1 row. |

## Initiative 18 — `csproj-reserialization-msbuildworkspace` `schedule: heroic-last`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Initiative id** | `csproj-reserialization-msbuildworkspace` |
| **Status** | `pending` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `create-file-apply-csproj-side-effect-all-projects` (P2) |
| **Diagnosis** | **CRITICAL destructive side-effect.** Verified at `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/RefactoringService.cs:691-840`: `PersistDocumentSetChangesAsync` calls `_workspace.TryApplyChanges(workspaceId, modifiedSolution)` at line 793. The v1.18.2 Item #5 fix snapshots **only SDK-style csprojs that have ADDED documents** (line 716-737) and restores them at line 801-824. The P2 row reports a DIFFERENT scope: ALL 40 csproj files get BOM (`\ufeff`) inserted, blank lines stripped between `<PropertyGroup>`/`<ItemGroup>`, and CRLF→LF conversion — even csprojs with NO document changes. Root cause: MSBuildWorkspace's internal `MSBuild.Construction.ProjectRootElement` save logic runs on every project in the workspace when `TryApplyChanges` commits, normalizing files even when they have no semantic edits. The Item #5 snapshot only covers `addedDocuments.Count > 0` projects. |
| **Approach** | Extend the Item #5 snapshot pattern to **every csproj in the workspace** before `TryApplyChanges`. After the call, restore any csproj whose disk content differs ONLY in normalization (BOM, line-endings, blank-line collapsing). Use a content-comparison helper that ignores trivia: parse XML on both sides, compare semantically. If only-trivia diff: restore original bytes. If semantic diff: keep the new bytes (legitimate change like the Item #5 path). Alternative approach: investigate Roslyn's `MSBuildWorkspace.SkipUnchangedFiles` or equivalent setting; if such an option exists, opt in. |
| **Scope** | `RefactoringService.cs` (extend snapshot loop) + new XML-equivalent comparison helper (`Helpers/CsprojSemanticEquality.cs`?) + tests. ~3-4 files. **Larger if Roslyn-internals investigation reveals a setting that achieves the same end with one line.** |
| **Risks** | (a) Snapshotting all csprojs adds I/O proportional to project count — measure on Jellyfin (40 projects). May need to do a `dotnet build` style hash comparison rather than full read. (b) The XML semantic-equivalence helper must be conservative — if uncertain, keep new bytes (don't risk silently undoing legitimate changes). (c) Some legitimate apply paths DO modify all csprojs (e.g., `migrate_package_preview` apply across central package management) — the comparison must handle these correctly. (d) Heroic scheduling: this initiative has the highest investigation risk; if root cause turns out to be a Roslyn-internal setting, the fix is small; if not, the fix may be substantial. |
| **Validation** | Critical regression test: invoke `create_file_apply` for ONE file in ONE project of a 40-project solution; assert `git diff --stat` shows only the new file (zero modifications to other csprojs). Re-run the Jellyfin §9.3 reproduction explicitly. |
| **Performance review** | **Hot path — measure.** Baseline: P2 row reports the destructive write itself happens during `TryApplyChanges`; no time impact reported. Post-fix: snapshot/compare adds I/O proportional to project count. Measure on Jellyfin 40-project solution; expected overhead <500 ms (40 csproj reads + parses). If higher, optimize the comparison helper. |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Fixed` |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **`*_apply` no longer reserializes unrelated csprojs (`create-file-apply-csproj-side-effect-all-projects`).** Critical destructive side-effect (Jellyfin experimental promotion 2026-04-16 §9.3): `create_file_apply` for one file in one project triggered solution-wide rewrite of all 40 csprojs (BOM insertion, blank-line stripping, CRLF→LF conversion). Distinct from the v1.18.2 Item #5 fix which addressed only SDK-style projects with added documents — this is MSBuildWorkspace-wide reserialization on every `TryApplyChanges`. Extended the snapshot pattern in `RefactoringService.PersistDocumentSetChangesAsync` to capture every csproj before `TryApplyChanges`; post-call, csprojs whose XML differs only in trivia (BOM, line-endings, whitespace) are restored from their pre-apply bytes. Semantic XML diff via new `CsprojSemanticEquality` helper. Regression test on Jellyfin 40-project solution: `create_file_apply` for one file produces `git diff --stat` showing only the new file. |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 1 row. P2 cleared. |
